text: Shipboard contingency plans prepared for emergencies must identify hazards and systematically include:

options.text: Allocation of specific emergency duties and responsibilities
Immediate actions to be taken to regain control of the situation
Communication methods within the ship and to outside authorities

explanation: Effective emergency management relies on a structured approach as mandated by the ISM Code. Contingency plans must define roles to avoid confusion, outline immediate mitigation steps to prevent escalation, and provide clear communication protocols for internal coordination and external reporting (e.g., to the shore, MRCC).
